message:
refactor(list): consolidate full-scan list relation lookups into one map

Folds the three separate full-scan queries (`get_all_labels`,
`count_all_relation_counts` returning a (deps, dependents) tuple of
maps) used by `br list` into a single helper that returns one
`HashMap<issue_id, ListRelationMetadata>`. Per-issue mapping then does
one HashMap::remove instead of three lookups across three maps.

src/storage/sqlite.rs (+118 lines, no deletions):

- New `pub(crate) struct ListRelationMetadata { labels, dependency_count,
  dependent_count }` next to the WAL constants. Default is the empty
  metadata (no labels, both counts zero), so `unwrap_or_default()` at
  the call site gives the correct shape for issues with no relations.
  Derives Debug/Clone/Default/PartialEq/Eq for testing.
- `pub(crate) fn get_all_list_relation_metadata(&self)
  -> Result<HashMap<String, ListRelationMetadata>>`: runs the same
  three SQL scans the old helpers ran (labels ordered by
  issue_id+label, dependency_counts grouped by issue_id, dependent_counts
  grouped by depends_on_id), then folds them into one map keyed by
  issue_id. Capacity is pre-sized as
  `label_rows.len() + dep_rows.len() + dependent_rows.len()` (saturating
  add) so we don't reallocate on the population pass; this is an upper
  bound (some issue ids appear in more than one of the three result
  sets), but a tight one. Empty / zero values are skipped at write time
  so the map's HashMap::Default state stays meaningful.

src/cli/commands/list.rs:

- The `use_full_relation_scan` branch previously called
  `get_all_labels()` and `count_all_relation_counts()` separately,
  returning three maps that were then zipped per issue with three
  lookups. Now it calls `get_all_list_relation_metadata()` and does
  one `relation_metadata.remove(&issue.id).unwrap_or_default()` per
  issue, which yields the labels Vec and both counts in one shot.
- The non-full-scan branch (when `should_use_full_relation_scan`
  returns false) is unchanged in behavior — it still calls
  `get_labels_for_issues(&issue_ids)` and
  `count_relation_counts_for_issues(&issue_ids)` because the small-
  set targeted query path is faster than the full scan when the
  caller has already narrowed down to specific issue IDs.
- The two arms now produce `Vec<IssueWithCounts>` directly rather
  than going through a separate "convert to IssueWithCounts" step
  outside the if/else, which removes one Vec-build and clarifies
  control flow.

Net effect on full-list output (the common case for `br list`,
`br list --json`, etc.): one fewer pass over `issues`, one fewer
HashMap allocation, and the per-issue hot loop drops from three
HashMap probes to one HashMap::remove. SQL traffic is unchanged
(same three queries hit the database in the same order).

No call-site change for the legacy helpers `get_all_labels` /
`count_all_relation_counts` — they're still used elsewhere
(viewer, JSON dumps), so they stay public on SqliteStorage.
